Kevin,
 
Per our conversation, enclosed please find Artists First writer material in consideration for HOUSEBROKEN staffing. Please take a look and let us know what you think.
 
UPPER LEVEL
 
ALYSON FOUSE
Alyson recently was a Co-Ep on the CBS sitcom THE NEIGHBORHOOD, starring Max Greenfield and Cedric The Entertainer. She previously wrote on BIG SHOT, now streaming on Disney+.  Previously she wrote for Fox’s THE COOL KIDS as a consulting producer, and for Freeform’s upcoming BESTIES.  She began her television career working closely with Keenen Ivory Wayans on IN LIVING COLOR and THE KEENEN IVORY WAYANS SHOW. This led to a string of successful writing and producing credits including COMEDY GET DOWN, UNCLE BUCK, MY WIFE AND KIDS, THE WANDA SYKES SHOW and EVERYBODY HATES CHRIS, for which she received a WGA nomination. She wrote the first two films in the SCARY MOVIE franchise and four films in the BRING IT ON franchise. Alyson wrote a  ½ hour comedy for Fox starring stand-up comedian Ms. Pat, as well as did all the joke punch-ups for GIRLS TRIP and PITCH PERFECT 3.  She wrote and co-executive produced ALL ABOUT THE WASHINGTONS for Netflix, which was released last summer, and was a co-executive producer on Amazon’s upcoming A LEAGUE OF THEIR OWN.
 
LAURA KRAFFT
Laura Krafft was one of the original writers on Comedy Central’s THE COLBERT REPORT, where she wrote for three seasons and won an Emmy for Outstanding Writing.  She is an upper level comedy writer currently staffed on SAUSAGE PARTY (Amazon/Annapurna/Point Grey).  Previously, she has written on CALL ME KAT (WBTV/Fox), the animated show HARPER HOUSE for CBS All Access, the CBS series MURPHY BROWN, the FOX series GRANDFATHERED starring John Stamos, Comedy Central’s ANOTHER PERIOD and FX’s THE COMEDIANS.  She also wrote on two highly-acclaimed Larry Wilmore specials on Showtime, worked on CBS’s THE CRAZY ONES, and was handpicked to write and show run Armando Iannucci’s Comedy Central pilot TIME TRUMPET. Her writing has appeared in Vanity Fair, Tar Magazine, Bust Magazine, Anna David’s TRUE TALES OF LUST AND LOST, Arthur Jones’ POST-IT NOTE DIARIES, and Stephen Colbert’s I AM AMERICA (AND SO CAN YOU). She began her career performing in Chicago at Second City and Improv Olympic, near her hometown of Evanston. Attached please find THE FAVORITE (pilot) by Laura Krafft.
 
ELIOT GLAZER
Eliot is an LGBTQ writer and performer who most recently served as an executive producer on YouTube Premium’s original half-hour series, LIZA ON DEMAND, and wrote an episode of THE BOYS animated spinoff for Amazon.  He is developing and is attached to star opposite Arrow’s Colton Haynes, in the AMC Studio’s original, half-hour I RUN HOT with executive producer Ilana Glazer for Peacock. He is also developing the original half-hour, DENTATA, for Paramount TV and THE UNTITLED LIZA KOSHY MOVIE for Nickelodeon.  He wrote for Comedy Central's BROAD CITY and recurred throughout its run as Ilana’s brother, 'Eliot.’  He recently served as a supervising producer on the TV Land series, TEACHERS and as the showrunner of the MTV pilot, GAY CODE.  Other writing credits include; Fox's THE NEW GIRL, Darren Star's TV Land series YOUNGER, VH-1's BEST WEEK EVER and Comedy Central's TIME TRAVELING BONG.
 
Eliot has contributed to Funny or Die's BILLY ON THE STREET, Hulu's DIFFICULT PEOPLE and Bravo's ODD MOM OUT. He can be seen on the Nat Geo series DUCK QUACKS DON'T ECHO, the Hulu series DEADBEAT, on Comedy Central's @MIDNIGHT and starring in the Jax Media produced pilot, BLOOD, SWEETS AND TEARS. Eliot can regularly be seen performing live at HAUNTING RENDITIONS, his bi-coastal, monthly night of musical comedy and heard as a Howard Stern Wrap Up show guest. He can currently be seen in a supporting role in HBO Max’s AN AMERICAN PICKLE opposite Seth Rogen and on HGTV’s HOUSE HUNTERS: COMEDIANS ON COUCHES. Eliot’s original animated pilot, BATHWATER, is attached here
 
MID LEVEL
 
MARINA COCKENBERG
Marina just wrapped on the upcoming Netflix show MURDERTOWN and most recently wrote on the UNTITLED BALDWIN/GRAMMER PROJECT for ABC. She previously wrote for Mindy Kaling’s NEVER HAVE I EVER for Netflix and will return for season 3, and she has written for A LITTLE LATE WITH LILLY SINGH and THE TONIGHT SHOW STARRING JIMMY FALLON. She's received Emmy Awards for Outstanding Interactive Program, Outstanding Creative Achievement in Interactive Media, and ironically won "Best Car" in high school. She is co-creating an animated sow with Stephen Colbert’s Spartina Productions and CBS Studios. She has written and performed at CollegeHumor and Upright Citizens Brigade. She currently resides in Brooklyn. LOGOUT written by Marina Cockenberg attached. 
 
ARI BERKOWITZ
Accomplished in both the feature & television space, Ari just joined the staff of Disney+’s THE SANTA CLAUSE and has staffed on CAROL’S SECOND ACT, Lord & Miller’s Netflix animated series HOOPS, IT’S ALWAYS SUNNY IN PHILADELPHIA and RYAN HANSEN SOLVES CRIMES ON TELEVISION. She previously developed the pilot VALLEY GIRLS at ABC based on the book GEEK GIRL RISING about women in Silicon Valley and previously developed at Disney+. 
 
On the feature side she is co-writing LETTERS TO SANTA with Hello Sunshine producing for Paramount, an animated Christmas movie SUPER FRIENDS for Warner Brothers Animation and adapting the novel WOULD LIKE TO MEET for TriStar, alongside her feature writing partner Ava Tramer. The duo teamed up and wrote the 2018 Blacklist script PLEASE MATERNITY LEAVE.  A WHOVILLE WHODUNNIT written by Ari Berkowitz
 
 
LOWER LEVEL
 
RACHEL HASTINGS
Rachel just wrapped as Story Editor on Season 2 of Apple’s animated musical series CENTRAL PARK. The show premiered in 2020 to incredible reviews and is certified fresh on Rotten Tomatoes. Prior to that, she worked on staff for the Cartoon Network project THE FUNGIES. Rachel got her start on FOX’s BOB’S BURGERS in its second season. Starting out working in production and writing for the show's comic book, she received her own episode in Season 7. Rachel is currently developing an animated series with Big Breakfast and a holiday feature with DiNovi & Muse Entertainment. Her pilot I HATE PUPPETS is attached as a sample.
 
KELLY LANDRY
Kelly recent sold development to CBS Studios and was the show runner on the animated Quibi series YOUR DAILY HOROSCOPE with Will Arnett & ATTN: Media producing. Kelly served as head writer/director for the 2018 comedy series, STARTER PACK, which follows Ayana Martin, a broke 25-year-old with a master’s degree in art history, as she sets out to curate a meme museum. The show explores themes around the ownership of digital art, and the concept of meme-ing responsibly. The New York Times reviewed the show as a “bright and poppy fictional series that reads like an aesthetic mash-up of Instagram videos and MTV reality shows,” and deemed it one of the best shows streaming on Facebook Watch. Prior to STARTER PACK, Kelly wrote, co-directed and produced AT&T Hello Lab’s 2017 venture, GUILTY PARTY, a multi-platform digital series. The show blew past industry benchmarks garnering over 65 million views across four platforms. CARMEN’S A BITCH written by Kelly Landry attached as sample. 
 
MARCELINA CHAVIRA
Marcelina was conceived during a ham fueled moment of passion over an Easter Holiday weekend in Eagle Pass, Texas. She spent her formative years in Dallas scaring her friends’ very conservative and very white parents by proclaiming that Jesus is Black and God is a woman. At Bryn Mawr College she studied dance and anthropology. Shortly thereafter she received an MA from NYU in Performance Studies. She then decided against a Ph.D. and moved to L.A. to become a cartoon.  But cartoons are not real. So she resigned herself to voice over and has since found a wonderful home in sketch comedy.  Marcelina is constantly writing for and performing with the all women of color sketch team The BAE*GENCY, The Pack Theater house sketch team Friendo, and the all Latinx sketch team The Latinx Comedy Pachanga. She recently was in the writers room with a few of her comedy heroes and Groundlings Alumni for Your Late Night Show Tonight with Drew Droege with special guest Fortune Feimster.  Marcelina just wrapped on the animated show BROBOTS for Netflix and Reel FX. 
  
MACHO LIBRE (pilot) by Marcelina Chavira. After his fathers pass away, a mature and woke AF 11 year-old boy is forced to move in with his estranged, old school, super macho, ex-luchador grandfather above the aging man’s eccentric wrestling academy. This imaginative pilot is being produced by Mandeville TV and was included on The Black List’s inaugural Latinx TV List. Catalina Aguilar-Mastretta is attached to direct.
 
HUNTER TORO
Hunter Toro is a LatinX writer and improv comedian from the lower back tattoo of the United States, Florida.  She grew up near the swamps, where she’d pull her brother out of quicksand and watch her dad dump the corpses of beloved pets for the gators to take care of.  Hunter attended UNC Chapel Hill and has since been selected for the 2020-2021 Black List x Women In Film Episodic Lab and the 2021 Nickelodeon Writing Program.  She is currently staffed on the hybrid scripted/unscripted series, TRUE STORY (Peacock/Pacific Electric) that Ed Helms and Randall Park are co-hosting.
WONKA (pilot) by Hunter Toro.  In the aftermath of the golden ticket fiasco, Willy Wonka’s assistant must save the factory from Wonka himself.
We look forward to hearing your thoughts!
 
Best,
Haley
 
Haley Jones 
Artists First
9465 Wilshire Blvd | Ste 900 | Beverly Hills, CA 90212
